fresh_facts.claim: The US deployed a third aircraft carrier strike group led by USS George HW Bush plus thousands of troops to the Middle East as a pressure tactic in relation to Iran negotiations.
President Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran on April 21, 2026 but rejected further truce extensions and warned of potential assault.
The US halted approximately $500 million in dollar shipments to Iraq and froze security cooperation to curb Iran-backed militias after IRGC commander Esmail Qaani's Baghdad visit.
Iran unveiled advanced missiles including the Korumshar 4 with 2,000 km range and GA Michelle, signaling military preparedness amid US pressure.
The IRGC warned Gulf nations of oil production risks and claimed control of the Strait of Hormuz despite US blockade, with approximately 20 vessels reported breaching the blockade.
Iran claims its forces are at approximately 70% of pre-war strength with underground missile and drone replenishment capabilities.
Iran's Revolutionary Guards seized two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz on April 18, 2026, hours after President Trump announced a ceasefire extension.
Iran reversed a Hormuz reopening by firing on a tanker and reimposing a blockade on April 18, 2026, signaling strategic posturing ahead of diplomacy.
The fragile US-Iran ceasefire is set to expire on April 22, 2026, with mounting opposition in Tehran to new US talks in Islamabad.
Iran's deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh stated on April 18 that no date for talks is set until a 'framework' is agreed upon.

ISW's live tracker as of February 3, 2026 documents severe regime instability from prior protests with indicators including regime framing of protesters as 'terrorists' and deployment of approximately 800-5,000 Iranian-backed Iraqi militias.
Khamenei issued an order on January 9, 2026 for lethal crackdowns against protesters that persists amid internet blackouts and Starlink confiscations.
Senior Iranian officials including LEC Commander Radan and Ali Larijani met with Chinese and Russian counterparts regarding repression assistance.
Iranian security forces have been arrested for refusing to fire on protesters, according to ISW reporting as of February 3, 2026.
Iran executed a former atomic agency employee for alleged Israeli spying on an unspecified recent date prior to April 22, 2026.
Experts at an Iran International townhall warned that Iran's economy could collapse faster than the US or global economy under war, sanctions, US blockade, and Hormuz disruptions.
Iranian officials including Mohseni-Ejei warned of 'savage aggression' and new battlefield 'cards' while branding US actions an 'act of war.'
The IRGC's Majid Mousavi highlighted reserve missiles in a video released around April 19, 2026.
Pakistan talks were stalled due to US vessel seizures according to reports from April 20, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces an acute multi-front crisis as the April 22 ceasefire expires with the US deploying a third carrier strike group and 10,000+ troops, while Tehran simultaneously confronts internal instability marked by protest crackdowns, security force defections, and accelerating economic collapse from sanctions and Hormuz blockade pressures. Iran has signaled military escalation options through advanced missile unveilings and Strait of Hormuz provocations, while rejecting diplomatic frameworks and warning of 'savage aggression' response.
regime_fall.last_major_event: The US-Iran ceasefire expired on April 22, 2026, with the US deploying a third aircraft carrier strike group (USS George HW Bush) and 10,000+ additional troops to the Middle East, while simultaneously halting $500 million in dollar shipments to Iraq and freezing security cooperation to counter Iran-backed militias, as Iran unveiled advanced missile capabilities and warned of military escalation.
